seoTitle: Remove or Delete a Microsoft Teams Account
metaDescription: Disconnect a device profile, leave a guest organization, or fully close a personal Microsoft account tied to Teams, step by step.
intro: Removing a Teams profile means something different depending on whether you're disconnecting a device, leaving someone else's organization, or fully closing a personal Microsoft account. This guide separates those three scenarios so you clear the right data without breaking accounts you still need.

steps.heading: Check which accounts are active
steps.body: Click your profile avatar to review the sign-in profiles currently connected to Teams.

steps.heading: Leave a guest organization
steps.body: Open app settings, go to Accounts and orgs, and click Leave Org next to any third-party guest network you want to exit.

steps.heading: Disconnect a work or school account from your device
steps.body: In your operating system settings, go to Accounts, select Access work or school, choose the account, and click Disconnect.

steps.heading: Close a personal account entirely
steps.body: If you need a full data wipe, sign in through the official account closure dashboard in your browser and verify your identity with a security token.

steps.heading: Clear local caches
steps.body: Run an application cache clean afterward to force Teams to fully recognize the removed profile.

faq.question: What's the difference between disconnecting and closing an account?
faq.answer: Disconnecting removes a device-level link to a work or school account, while closing permanently deletes a personal Microsoft account and its data.

faq.question: Do I need admin rights to remove my own Teams account?
faq.answer: Leaving a guest organization or disconnecting a device account doesn't require admin rights, but enterprise-managed account removal typically does.

faq.question: Will closing my personal account delete all my Teams data?
faq.answer: Yes, closing a personal Microsoft account triggers a full data wipe across connected services, including Teams.

faq.question: Why does Teams still show my old account after I remove it?
faq.answer: Cached credentials can linger — clearing your system keychain or app cache usually resolves lingering sign-in prompts.
